activity.title: ✨ Unexpected Surprises from the Bugcrowd CTF 2025! ✨ From 6th to 7th August 2025, I had the opportunity to participate in the Bugcrowd CTF 2025, where over 2000 talented hackers from around the world came together to test their skills. I’m thrilled to share that I scored 1960 points, securing a position under the Top 100! 🏅 The best part? I recently received an official Bugcrowd Challenge Coin as a token of recognition — an unexpected and wonderful surprise that made the experience even more memorable! 💫 This CTF was a great mix of thrill, learning, and persistence — from tackling exploitation and reversing challenges to testing creative thinking under pressure. Every problem pushed me to explore new areas of cybersecurity and sharpen my skills. A huge shoutout to Bugcrowd for organizing such an amazing event and for recognizing participants’ efforts in such a meaningful way. 🙌 Here’s to more challenges, learning, and surprises ahead! 🚀 #CTF #CyberSecurity #Bugcrowd #EthicalHacking #CaptureTheFlag #LearningByDoing #InfosecCommunity #pentest #cybersec #learning ...more

It’s been a while since I posted an update, but apart from diving deep into Software Exploitation, Maldev, Backend dev I recently made this cool project that I'm really excited to share — a BOT 🤖 As a huge fan of KSI, I’ve always loved his “Try Not to Laugh” series — especially the part where every time he laughs, he drops Amazon gift card coupons worth $100 to $2000 💸 So I thought: “Why not automate this?” Introducing 👉 ksi_tnl_coupon_automation 🧠 What It Does: • Download “Try Not to Laugh” videos using yt-dlp tool (sends an alert on mail too) • Extracts all the text in the video per frame using Google Video Intelligence API . • Extracts all the coupons from the text using regex. Sends the found coupons directly to your Gmail inbox! And in the final version, it even uses Selenium WebDriver to auto-claim the coupon on Amazon! 📦 Technologies Used: • 🧠 Google Cloud Video Intelligence API (for detecting text from video) • ☁️ Google Cloud Storage (to store and analyze videos) • 🖥️ Selenium WebDriver (for automating coupon claiming) • ✉️ Python Mail API (to send coupon codes to Gmail) • 💻 Python (core logic + backend) • 🧠 And of course, lots of regex and parsing logic to extract coupon codes 😅 Fun Fact: While testing, I discovered (via Reddit) that YouTube’s review team or Moe himself might be claiming the coupons before the video even goes public 😂 Whether it’s a conspiracy or not… I had a blast building this bot! If you're into bots, automation, or just want to see how I pulled this off, I’ve documented the full setup in the README.md on GitHub. 🔍 What I Learned: 1. Web Automation with Selenium 2. Working with Google Cloud APIs 3. Importance of researching your idea before implementing it 😅 And how to handle real-world API limitations and edge cases 𝗚̲𝗼̲𝗼̲𝗴𝗹̲𝗲̲ ̲𝗮̲𝗹̲𝘀̲𝗼̲ ̲𝗼̲𝗳̲𝗳̲𝗲̲𝗿̲𝘀̲ ̲$̲3̲0̲0̲ ̲𝗳̲𝗿̲𝗲̲𝗲̲ ̲𝗰̲𝗿̲𝗲̲𝗱̲𝗶̲𝘁̲𝘀̲ ̲𝗳̲𝗼̲𝗿̲ ̲𝗮̲𝗻̲𝘆𝗼̲𝗻̲𝗲̲ ̲𝗴𝗲̲𝘁̲𝘁̲𝗶̲𝗻̲𝗴 ̲𝘀̲𝘁̲𝗮̲𝗿̲𝘁̲𝗲̲𝗱̲ ̲𝘄̲𝗶̲𝘁̲𝗵̲ ̲𝗚̲𝗖̲𝗣̲ — I used it̲ ̲t̲o̲ ̲po̲w̲e̲r̲ ̲t̲h̲i̲s̲ ̲e̲n̲t̲i̲r̲e̲ ̲pr̲o̲je̲c̲t. Highly recommend taking advantage of it if you're learning cloud tech! Would love to hear your thoughts, feedback, or improvements. Feel free to check out the project and let me know what crazy things you'd automate next 😄👇 🔗 https://lnkd.in/geU4tQbK #𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 #𝗰𝗹𝗼𝘂𝗱𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 #𝗴𝗼𝗼𝗴𝗹𝗲𝗰𝗹𝗼𝘂𝗱 #𝗯𝗼𝘁 #𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗼𝘂𝗿𝗰𝗲 #𝗸𝘀𝗶 #𝘃𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗼𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴 #𝗳𝘂𝗻𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁 #python #𝘀𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗻𝗶𝘂𝗺 ...more

On 31st August📅, I had the incredible opportunity to participate in the Escalatex Prescreening, where I secured 45th place🏁, qualifying for the second round. This marked the beginning of my first offline CTF experience at GLA University, Mathura, organized by the amazing Cyberonites Club team🧑‍💻. The event was well-structured, and the atmosphere was charged with excitement. I tackled challenges in Pawning🚩, Reversing🔀, and OSINT🤖, which were both engaging and intellectually stimulating. Through determination and problem-solving, I was able to score 1100 points and secure 11th place. It was an enriching experience, not only for the competition itself but also for the collaboration and learning that took place. Overall, I must say that it was an absolutely wonderful experience and a commendable initiative by Cyberonites Club at GLA University , Mathura. The event truly stands out as a great milestone in my journey! ...more

Yesterday, I had the exhilarating experience of participating in a Jeopardy-style CTF hosted by CyGenix Security online. This 24-hour 🕰️event featured a thrilling array of 24 CTF challenges covering diverse topics such as pwn 🚩, reverse engineering, OSINT 🤖, forensic , web🕸️, and miscellaneous. I successfully conquered 10 of these challenges, but my personal highlight was the pwn challenge, where I had to exploit a binary to gain access to a shell or retrieve a flag.txt. It was a truly invigorating experience, and I can't wait to share my detailed write-up on the challenges I conquered. 🎯 https://lnkd.in/g2UfqHXW #cybersec #ctfs #iwantmorechallengelikethis ...more

Meet Rikugan, a reverse engineering agent that lives inside your disassembler. Over the past few months, I’ve been studying LLMs more in depth, and one of the things I built from that work is an IDA Pro and Binary Ninja plugin that brings agentic reversing into your workflow. This is not an MCP, it’s a full agent, with an agentic loop, a context manager, subagents orchestration for binary exploration, skills and many more. Check it out: https://lnkd.in/drUuHRJE https://lnkd.in/drn8_57V
This article walks through the design and implementation of a DPI engine in C++, focusing on protocol parsing, SNI extraction, and traffic filtering.

I just earned my first $𝟭,𝟬𝟬𝟬 ( ₹𝟵𝟬𝗸 ) from code. 💸 By winning an 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗔𝗜 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗲𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻... 𝗦𝗢𝗟𝗢 💪. Hosted by Baidu, Inc. I’m a 3rd-year college student. With just a laptop and a problem most models fail at. I won Baidu, Inc. 𝗘𝗥𝗡𝗜𝗘 𝗔𝗜 𝗗𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗹𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗿 𝗖𝗵𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗻𝗴𝗲 against teams from around the world (hosted on Devpost). 🏆 𝗕𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗣𝗮𝗱𝗱𝗹𝗲𝗢𝗖𝗥-𝗩𝗟 𝗙𝗶𝗻𝗲-𝗧𝘂𝗻𝗲 𝗔𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗱 💰 $𝟭,𝟬𝟬𝟬 𝗽𝗿𝗶𝘇𝗲 The problem wasn’t beginner-friendly. Millions of 16th-century Spanish manuscripts are still effectively locked away because they’re unreadable: • extreme calligraphy • ink bleed • archaic language • chaotic layouts Standard OCR systems break here (~20% error). 𝗜 𝗿𝗲𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗲𝗱 𝗶𝘁 𝘁𝗼 𝟭.𝟲𝟰%. I built 𝗖𝗵𝗿𝗼𝗻𝗼𝘀-𝗩𝗟, a vision-language pipeline bridging a 500-year gap between a scribe in 1545 and a researcher in 2025. Key results: Character Error Rate: 𝟭𝟵.𝟴𝟮% → 𝟭.𝟲𝟰% Usability: ~𝟭% → 𝟳𝟳% How it worked: • Fine-tuned PaddleOCR-VL on NVIDIA A100s rented via Vast.ai • Built a normalization engine for archaic → modern Spanish • Solved hallucination and training instability issues Thanks to the judges and the PaddlePaddle team at Baidu, Inc. for building infrastructure where individual builders can compete globally. P.S My work got recognized by the ERNIE Team and I got an email regarding collaboration ✨. (Thats a win too 🥳) This isn’t just prize money. It’s proof that a student working alone can move a problem that’s been stuck for years. Project is open-source. Demo below. Back to building 🔥.

Built an agentic AI system that lives on X (Twitter) and talks to people for me. 𝗬𝗲𝘀. 𝗢𝗻 𝗽𝘂𝗿𝗽𝗼𝘀𝗲. This project started as 👉 “lemme just fetch some tweets” and somehow turned into a full-blown 𝗫 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 & 𝗔𝘂𝘁𝗼-𝗥𝗲𝗽𝗹𝘆 𝗔𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁. What this thing actually does (no buzzwords, promise) : • OAuth 2.0 (PKCE) auth with X (aka: pain, but secure) • Pulls recent posts + mentions • Runs NLP-based filtering to separate real opportunities from crypto spam • Classifies post intent & tone (question, complaint, promo, professional, etc.) • Auto-posts replies using the X API • All controlled through a React dashboard Basically : less marketing revenue, more automation 🎉. less manual replying, more leverage. ⚙️ 𝗧𝗲𝗰𝗵 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗰𝗸: • Backend: Flask, SQLAlchemy, SQLite • Frontend: React (Vite), React Router • AI: 1. LangChain + Gemini 2.ML : TF-IDF + cosine similarity • Infra reality checks: sessions, tokens, rate limits, edge cases 😮‍💨 💥 What I actually learned building this: OAuth is never “done” — it just stops breaking for now 😭 In-memory state is a trap (learned that the hard way , tho still using it) Agents need constraints, not just prompts “It works locally” means nothing without real flows (fr) Clean pipelines > clever hacks This isn’t a toy project. It’s modular, extendable, and ready to scale with: • token refresh • background jobs • persistent auth • multi-user support Still iterating. Still breaking things. Still shipping. 𝗗𝗲𝗺𝗼 : https://lnkd.in/gSnAu8Mk 𝗥𝗲𝗽𝗼: https://lnkd.in/guQm8TMk If you’re into AI agents, automation, or building systems that think before they speak, I’d love feedback (or chaos). Would also like to give a huge thanks to Mridul Chaudhary for mentoring me in every phase of this project as an amazing senior 🎊 Shipping > polishing. Always. #BuildInPublic #AgenticAI #AIEngineering #FullStackDev #Automation #XAPI #SystemsThinking #GenZDev #StudentDeveloper #LearningInPublic #CodeNewbie #DeveloperJourney

From observing to leading — 12 months that changed me. Today, I complete one year at TechDefence . (5 months as an intern, 7 months as a full-time Project Coordinator) When I entered TechDefence, I was honestly confused, unsure, and still trying to understand where I truly fit. I had knowledge, ambition, and pressure — but not clarity. And then… work started. 📅 16 December 2024 Deadlines. Responsibilities. Expectations. No time to overthink. When you don’t fully understand operations, you either panic — or you learn fast and stand strong. That was my first real test. And also my first real lesson in the professional world. What made the difference was support. Support to learn. Support to make mistakes. Support to grow beyond comfort. I was encouraged to think differently, to step outside the box, and to build my foundation — starting with ISO 27001 certification. Between December and March, I learned how things actually work: Compliance, audits, VAPT, client handling, escalations, new project initiation — and the basics of project management in cybersecurity. Then came March — another deadline. But this time, I wasn’t scared. I was prepared. I delivered on time, gave my 100% to my team, and saw a change in myself — better problem-solving, better communication, better ownership. 📌 April changed everything again. I got the opportunity to work closely with the SOC team, support new project onboarding, and understand real delivery pressure. Alongside work, I kept pushing myself: Completed NPTEL Project Management (IIT Roorkee) Cleared ALP Loco Pilot exam and appeared for CBT Designed a 5-phase project onboarding process, templates, and master trackers by applying what I was learning 📅 1st May — a tough personal decision I received my PPO. I had to choose between continuing exam paths or joining full-time. I chose responsibility, learning, and long-term growth. Since then, as a Project Coordinator – Cyber Security, I’ve worked across: SOC operations, GRC, compliance, product support, forensic projects — always focusing on smooth onboarding, customer experience, and delivery. 📚 In November, I completed ISO 42001 (AIMS). 🎤 I also delivered expert lectures at Rashtriya Raksha University Lucknow Campus , Research paper at an international workshop on Smart Policing, and learned from industry leaders at the PMI Gujarat Chapter Conference. Looking back, I realise something deeply personal: TechDefence didn’t just give me a role. It gave me stability. It gave me confidence. It gave me a way to support my family. It gave me a professional identity. I truly moved from observing to leading — step by step, mistake by mistake, lesson by lesson. Grateful for this journey. Grounded by this experience. Hungry to grow further in Cyber Security, Project Management, and GRC. This is only the beginning.Thankyou so much to All and my LinkedIn Community who support me , I always need your guidance and Support in my life.

𝐖𝐞 𝐰𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐈𝐇 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓!! 🚀🏆 Our team secured the winning position under Problem Statement ID 25191, proposed by the Ministry of Power & Power Grid Corporation of India Limited, focused on developing an AI/ML-enabled Digital Twin for EHV substations. EHV substations are highly complex environments containing transformers, reactors, circuit breakers, isolators, CTs, CVTs, busbars and advanced protection systems. Managing such critical assets requires real time visibility predictive insights and seamless coordination between field operations and control room systems. Traditional monitoring approaches often operate in silos and lack integrated intelligence. To address this challenge we built a full scale AI driven Digital Twin ecosystem that replicates the physical electrical and operational characteristics of a 400/220 kV substation. Our Solution: 🔹 𝐴𝑠𝑠𝑒𝑡-𝐿𝑒𝑣𝑒𝑙 𝟥𝘋 𝑀𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙𝑙𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑜𝑓 𝑡𝑟𝑎𝑛𝑠𝑓𝑜𝑟𝑚𝑒𝑟𝑠, 𝑏𝑟𝑒𝑎𝑘𝑒𝑟𝑠, 𝐶𝑇𝑠, 𝐶𝑉𝑇𝑠, 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑝𝑟𝑜𝑡𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝑟𝑒𝑙𝑎𝑦𝑠. 🔹 𝑈𝑛𝑖𝑓𝑖𝑒𝑑 𝐷𝑎𝑡𝑎 𝐼𝑛𝑔𝑒𝑠𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝑓𝑟𝑜𝑚 𝑠𝑒𝑛𝑠𝑜𝑟𝑠, 𝑆𝐶𝐴𝐷𝐴, 𝐼𝑜𝑇, 𝑎𝑛𝑑 ℎ𝑖𝑠𝑡𝑜𝑟𝑖𝑐𝑎𝑙 𝑑𝑎𝑡𝑎. 🔹 𝐴𝐼/𝑀𝐿 𝐴𝑛𝑎𝑙𝑦𝑡𝑖𝑐𝑠 𝑓𝑜𝑟 𝑎𝑛𝑜𝑚𝑎𝑙𝑦 𝑑𝑒𝑡𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛, 𝑓𝑎𝑖𝑙𝑢𝑟𝑒 𝑝𝑟𝑒𝑑𝑖𝑐𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛, 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑜𝑝𝑒𝑟𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑎𝑙 𝑜𝑝𝑡𝑖𝑚𝑖𝑧𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛. 🔹 𝑆𝑖𝑚𝑢𝑙𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝐸𝑛𝑔𝑖𝑛𝑒 𝑡𝑜 𝑡𝑒𝑠𝑡 𝑠𝑡𝑟𝑒𝑠𝑠 𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑑𝑖𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑠 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑓𝑎𝑢𝑙𝑡 𝑠𝑐𝑒𝑛𝑎𝑟𝑖𝑜𝑠. 🔹 𝐹𝑖𝑒𝑙𝑑 𝑂𝑝𝑒𝑟𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑠 𝐴𝑝𝑝 𝑓𝑜𝑟 𝑟𝑒𝑎𝑙-𝑡𝑖𝑚𝑒 𝑚𝑎𝑖𝑛𝑡𝑒𝑛𝑎𝑛𝑐𝑒 𝑡𝑎𝑠𝑘𝑠 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑑𝑎𝑡𝑎 𝑠𝑦𝑛𝑐 𝑤𝑖𝑡ℎ 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑡𝑤𝑖𝑛. Leading this team was an intense learning experience — from system design and AI integration to decision-making under real-world constraints. Huge thanks to Santosh Sir for his constant mentorship and technical guidance throughout the journey. Grateful to MLRITM for hosting us and providing an excellent environment for innovation. Special thanks to Om Sharma for standing by me throughout the journey and giving it his absolute best. Proud to have led this incredible team: Om S. | Manan Gakkhar | Rohit Sharma | Prachi Sharma | Sanchita Sharma Narendra Modi | Prime Minister's Office | Ministry of Education, Government of India, New Delhi | AICTE | All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) #DigitalTwin #EHVSubstation #AI #MachineLearning #PowerSystems #GridAutomation #SCADA #IoT #PredictiveMaintenance #AssetMonitoring #AnomalyDetection #FailurePrediction #SubstationAutomation #PowerGrid #MinistryofPower #SmartAutomation #EnergyTechnology #TransformerMonitoring #RealTimeAnalytics #UtilityTech #SmartEnergy

Nothing is Never Not Worth Trying. Well that got proved in my life last week, when me and my team won the Smart India Hackathon 2025! Writing this was so difficult for me as I literally am still shocked and out of words. The extreme hard work, dedication, sleepless nights and superb team work all led to this huge achievement in my life. This cheque does not only mean a cash prize, but a dream that I was chasing for almost 3 years come true. The moment our names were announced, I was in awe and shock. Thinking is this me dreaming? But thankfully it was not, I was officially a SIH Winner. We basically built a smart solution for agriculture and irrigation, especially for the state of Sikkim. Our solution consisted of smartly chosen sensors, designs, 3D models and other hardware, integrated with a software that was aimed at making a farmer's life easier. This idea came to life and became a proper product in just 5 days, only because our team worked tirelessly day and night. Abhishek Singh Chauhan , Ayush Kumar Jha , Sneha Mukherjee , Aditya Goel and Nikunj Sharma - every single member did a commendable job and worked as a team to achieve this. But apart from teamwork, another factor that was hugely responsible for our win, was our mentor - Vaibhav Khanna . Bhaiya all the words might be less to thank you and being the part of our team and giving your valuable time to us. Special thanks to the MUJ Team - Khetul Patel , Navam Agarwal , Khushi Rathore , Vaishnavi Chitme , Anuvi Kanthariya - had the best time with y'all😎 I still remember starting my college in 2023, not going to an IIT but to a lower tier college, I was not very happy with myself. I used to think that good college life, huge achievements, good friendships etc only existed there. But I am so glad that life chose to disprove me !! In the last 2.5 years I have made such great friends, have worked with such wonderful juniors and had experienced the guidance of mind-blowing seniors. Behind every one of my achievements and also behind this new and biggest one, stands something that I will never ever forget about, something that made me think that "Nahi yr apne college me bhi scope hai", a place where I have spent most of my college - Robogyan . I really want to give all credit to this one society and its seniors who were always just a call away from me at all times. Vidhi Gupta Di, Ashish ‎ Bhaiya, Vaibhav Khanna Bhaiya, Jayam Shrivastav Bhaiya and many more whom I might've not tagged here. Thank you with all my heart for encouraging me and helping me reach here in life! Also to the juniors and young minds reading this - do not stop trying and seriously do not stop having fun. Keep enjoying your life, if things are not what you wanted, give life some time. Maybe today's not your day, maybe you did everything right but still failed. But that is what learning is. Keep experimenting, keep making new friends and keep having fun. Grateful for the journey, the people, and the belief that kept me going.
